hooks.prompt: First check if .claude/docs-format exists in the project root. If it does NOT exist, this is not an Ultra Claude project — return {"ok": true} immediately. If it exists, check if the file being written/edited aligns with architecture docs in documentation/technology/architecture/. If that directory does not exist, return {"ok": true}. If it exists and the change contradicts documented architecture, return {"ok": false, "reason": "explanation of architectural violation"}. Return ONLY a valid JSON object.

First check if .claude/docs-format exists in the project root. If it does NOT exist, this is not an Ultra Claude project — return {"ok": true} immediately. If it exists, check if the task status in tool_input is being set to 'completed'. If not, return {"ok": true}. If it is being set to completed, check if the conversation context shows the task's success criteria (from the plan README.md) have been met. If criteria appear met or no plan is active, return {"ok": true}. If criteria are clearly unmet, return {"ok": false, "reason": "Task completion blocked: [specific unmet criteria]"}. Return ONLY a valid JSON object.

First check if .claude/docs-format exists in the project root. If it does NOT exist, this is not an Ultra Claude project — return {"ok": true} immediately. If it exists, check the stop_hook_active field in the input. If stop_hook_active is true, return {"ok": true} to prevent an infinite loop. Otherwise, check if the conversation involved active plan execution with in-progress tasks. If no plan execution is happening, return {"ok": true}. If plan execution was in progress, return {"ok": false, "reason": "Save checkpoint before stopping - there is in-progress plan execution"}. Return ONLY a valid JSON object, no other text.
